# mxroute_domain (Resource)

Manages a mail domain on the MXroute account. MXroute exposes no in-place update for a domain, so changing `domain` replaces the resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "mxroute_domain" "example" {
  domain = "example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String) The domain name to host mail for (e.g. `example.com`).

### Optional

- `mail_hosting` (Boolean) Whether mail hosting is enabled for the domain. Defaults to the value MXroute assigns on creation; set it explicitly to toggle mail hosting on or off.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier — the domain name.
- `pointers` (List of String) Domain pointers (aliases) that resolve to this domain.
- `ssl_enabled` (Boolean) Whether SSL is enabled for the domain. Server-managed via MXroute's AutoSSL, so this attribute is read-only (there is no API input to set it): it is typically `false` immediately after a domain is created and becomes `true` asynchronously — often within ~24 hours — once the certificate is issued.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# A domain is imported by its name.
terraform import mxroute_domain.example example.com
```
